name: Coffee — default suite
description: Real questions a curious coffee shopper might ask the recommender. Mix of short single-line, medium follow-ups, and long 2–3 sentence scenarios.

queries.query: I'm new to coffee — what equipment should I get to make better coffee than my drip machine?
Which espresso machine would work best for a busy household with two coffee drinkers?
I want to learn latte art at home — what tools and beans do I need to start?
How do I dial in a new bag of espresso beans without wasting half of them?
Decaf espresso that actually tastes like coffee — what should I look at?

We just moved into a new place and want to set up a small coffee bar. Our budget is around $2000 total and we drink mostly espresso drinks but occasionally pour-over on weekends. What machine, grinder, and accessories should we look at?

I've been pulling shots on my Breville for two years and I'm wondering if I'm missing something with single-origin beans. Can you walk me through how the same recipe tastes different across origins, and recommend a few starter beans to try?

My partner only drinks decaf but I want them to enjoy real espresso. Most decaf I've had tastes flat — are there decaf options that hold up in a double shot, and does the brewing technique need to change at all?

I make pour-over every morning but my coffee always tastes either too bitter or too sour. I'm using a V60 with medium-fine grind and 1:16 ratio. What variables should I be adjusting and in what order?

Looking for a coffee setup for our small office of about 10 people. We need something easy for everyone to use, makes consistent espresso drinks, and doesn't require a barista. Budget is flexible if it saves time.
